LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS LEE ROGERS AND PREMIER SALONS, INCORPORATED'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED. THERE IS A TRIABLE ISSUE WHETHER DEFENDANT WAS PLAINTIFF'S EMPLOYER. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. IN ADDITION, DEFENDANTS' COUNSEL MADE AN ORAL REQUEST THAT SHE BE ALLOWED TO REDACT DEFENDANTS' SOCIAL SECURITY NUMBERS CONTAINED IN PREVIOUSLY FILED DOCUMENTS. THERE WAS NO OBJECTION BY OPPOSING COUNSEL. THE REQUEST WAS GRANTED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN; COURT REPORTER: LAVENA WARD CSR #7077

LAW AND MOTION 301, NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANTS LEE ROGERS AND PREMIER SALONS, INCORPORATED'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED AS TO CAUSES OF ACTION 1, 2, 3, 7 AND 10. SUSTAINED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO CAUSES OF ACTION 5 AND 6 TO PLEAD A PERIOD OF EMPLOYMENT ENTITLING PLAINTIFF TO C F R A BENEFITS, AND AS TO CAUSES OF ACTION 8 TO PLEAD REFUSAL TO PARTICIPATE IN UNLAWFUL ACTIVITY. MOVING PARTY IS TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR # 8189 301/PJB

MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Rebecca H. Lessley, Esq., of the Lessley Law Firm, Tel. 626.578.1983, appearing for Defendant and moving party via court call.
Mary Shea Hagebols, Esq., of the Shea Law Offices, Tel. 510.208.4422, appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party.

RULING: THE MATTER WAS ARGUED. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS LEE ROGERS AND PREMIER SALONS, INCORPORATED'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION is Denied. There is a triable issue whether defendant was plaintiff's employer. Order signed in open Court. In addition, Defendants' counsel made an oral request that she be allowed to redact defendants' social security numbers contained in previously filed documents. There was no objection by opposing counsel. The request was granted. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Court Reporter: LaVena Ward CSR #7077
